title: Pin a module attribute at the right point
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.active_roles/0`. A module attribute `@roles ["admin", "editor", "viewer"]` defines the allowed values. The query should filter users whose `role` is in that list. Pin `@roles` correctly — do NOT inline the list into the query body, pin it once (`where: u.role in ^@roles`). Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Pinning a module attribute is subtly different from pinning a runtime variable — this is one of the patterns where `^` on a compile-time constant is actually justified (so Ecto parameterizes a list).
research_citation: LostKobrakai: 'The pin operator marks a value to be passed as a parameter to a query. If values change between executions, use it.'
